prompt:
You are a B2B prospecting analyst for a web agency. Analyze this locksmith's services page.

HOT LEAD signals:
- All services dumped on one page with no structure
- No individual service pages (bad for SEO)
- Missing service descriptions — just a bullet list
- No distinction between emergency and scheduled work
- No photos of actual work

COLD LEAD signals:
- Individual pages per service type (residential, commercial, auto, emergency)
- Detailed descriptions of what each service involves
- Clear emergency vs scheduled distinction
- Service-specific CTAs
- Before/after photos
- FAQ per service

Return each record as:
{
  "url": "<page url>",
  "pageName": "<short name>",
  "summary": "<1-2 sentence overview of services presentation>",
  "opportunityScore": "<1-10, higher = worse services page = better lead>",
  "serviceStructure": "<individual-pages|single-page|minimal|none>",
  "servicesListed": ["<service types found>"],
  "hasEmergencyDistinction": true/false,
  "hasServiceDescriptions": true/false,
  "topOpportunity": "<what a web agency could sell here>",
  "notes": "<one sentence prospecting assessment>"
}

---
You are analyzing web pages for the category described above.
For each page provided below, extract one record.
Follow the JSON schema from the category description exactly.

## Required fields (always include)
- "url": the page URL
- "pageName": short human-readable page name
- "summary": 1-2 sentence plain-language overview

## Field naming conventions (follow strictly)
- Scores: name ends with "Score", value is a number 1-10 (e.g. "conversionScore": 7)
- Booleans: name starts with "has"/"is" or ends with "Visible" (e.g. "pricingVisible": true)
- Short text: "notes", "description" — 1-2 sentences max
- Tags/lists: arrays of short strings (e.g. "classStyles": ["Hatha", "Vinyasa"])
- Plain strings: everything else (e.g. "price": "€15 drop-in")

Return a JSON object: { "records": [<one record per page>] }.
No markdown, no code fences.
response:
{
  "records": [
    {
      "url": "https://londonlocksmiths.com/services/",
      "pageName": "Services Overview",
      "summary": "All services are presented on a single page with short descriptive paragraphs per service. There is no structural separation between emergency and scheduled work, no individual service pages linked, and images appear to be stock rather than actual work.",
      "opportunityScore": 6,
      "serviceStructure": "single-page",
      "servicesListed": ["Lock Repairs", "Lock Replacements", "Property Entry", "Lock Picking"],
      "hasEmergencyDistinction": false,
      "hasServiceDescriptions": true,
      "topOpportunity": "Build out individual landing pages per service (residential, commercial, emergency, auto) with targeted SEO copy and service-specific CTAs to capture intent-driven search traffic.",
      "notes": "The page has more substance than a bare bullet list but still consolidates everything in one place with stock imagery, no emergency callout, and no segmentation by customer type — leaving significant SEO and conversion value on the table."
    },
    {
      "url": "https://londonlocksmiths.com/lock-repair/",
      "pageName": "Lock Repair",
      "summary": "A dedicated lock repair page exists with two descriptive sub-sections covering diagnosis and repair process. However, it relies entirely on stock photography, lacks FAQs, pricing signals, and any emergency vs. scheduled distinction despite claiming 24-hour availability.",
      "opportunityScore": 5,
      "serviceStructure": "individual-pages",
      "servicesListed": ["Lock Repair", "Lock Diagnosis"],
      "hasEmergencyDistinction": false,
      "hasServiceDescriptions": true,
      "topOpportunity": "Enhance the page with real job photography, an FAQ section, a prominent emergency CTA, and structured data markup to improve local search visibility and trust signals.",
      "notes": "The site shows partial maturity — individual service pages exist — but content depth, trust assets, and conversion optimisation are underdeveloped, making this a mid-tier opportunity for a web agency pitching SEO and CRO improvements."
    }
  ]
}
